Title: MY FPX Switcher
Content:
Last Update : Dec 30, 2026

MO PIC : Nora Atika Kon Nee Looi

Xendit Stakeholders

Department

PIC

PIC Role

Partnership

Musa Sharuddin

Partnership PIC for Shopeepay

Product

Zian Wei Lee

Chief Product Manager

Eligibility

Entity Type

Eligible

Local Domestic MY Entity Sdn Bhd / Bhd

✅

Local Domestic MY Entity Sole Prop

✅

Local Domestic MY Entity Association

✅

Local Domestic MY Entity LLP / Partnership

✅

Foreign Entity

✅

Local / Foreign Individual

❌


Merchant Requirements

Category

Requirements

Prerequisite (Local)

Local MY merchants must first be active and transacting under the Aggregator MID before being switched to their own dedicated MID.

SLA

Entity Origin

SLA

Foreign Entities

  • 14 {{glossary.working days}}

Local MY Entities

  • By default, local merchants are instantly activated via an Aggregator MID.

  • Once a merchant starts transacting, MO monitors their status via Databricks.

  • When ready, MO submits them to PayNet for their own Switcher MID.

Onboarding Flow

FPX Switcher for Foreign Entity

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

Channel Activation

1

Merchant submits "FPX" request via their dashboard; request appears in #mo-request.

Request Sample:

Merchant

Submission to Payex

2

MO fills FPX Submission Tracker (Columns to E). Use dummy email suffix in column C: malaysia.(tradename)@xendit.co

MO

3

The MO team will then manually submit the application to Payex. This is done using the Xendit Reference sign up link URL, utilizing the merchant details previously recorded in the FPX Submission Tracker & Spread Sheet.

4

For the Login Email and Confirm Login Email fields, please input the dummy email located in Column C. The format should follow: malaysia.(tradename)@xendit.co.

5

Always choose “Starter” Plan

6

Choose “FPX” for mode of payment

7

Then, fill the business details with (following the details in FPX Submission Tracker):

  • Type of Business Entity

  • Nature of Business

  • Registered Business Name

  • Trade Name

  • Business Registration Number

  • Registered Company Address

  • Business or Operation Address

8

After filling in all the merchant business details, MO will proceed to the next page to enter the 'Size of Company'

9

MO must then complete the Director Information section. Please ensure the Director Name is entered exactly as it appears on their NRIC (Malaysian ID).

You will also need to provide the NRIC number and Phone Number, following the specific details listed in the FPX Submission Tracker.

10

Next, MO needs to complete the Contact Person (CP) section. If the contact person is the same as the director, simply tick the checkbox for 'Same as Director’s Details.'

However, if the contact person is different, please input the appropriate information as specified in the FPX Submission Tracker..

11

MO will need to enter all relevant business information, including the merchant’s Website and Social Media handles. Additionally, please ensure to include the merchant's e-commerce URL (if available) to strengthen the application. All details should be cross-referenced with the information provided in the FPX Submission Tracker.

12

MO must then upload the merchant’s Legal Documents to verify their business identity. These files can be extracted directly from the Admin dashboard. Please ensure all documents are clear and legible before submitting, as per the details in the FPX Submission Tracker.

13

MO must then complete the Settlement Details using the following information:

  • Bank Name: United Overseas Bank

  • Bank Account Name: Xendit Malaysia Sdn Bhd

  • Bank Account Number: 2203042770

  • Notification Email: malaysia.(merchant name)@xendit.co

  • Confirm Notification Email: malaysia.(merchant name)@xendit.co

14

For the Notification Email and Confirm Notification Email, please use the exact same dummy email created during the first step (Login Email):

  • Format: malaysia.(merchant name)@xendit.co

15

In cases where the bank statement is not available in AD, MO may upload a blank page as a placeholder to proceed with the application.

This ensures the mandatory field is satisfied when Xendit’s internal data is being used for settlement instead.

16

Final Submission Steps:

  1. Tick the checkbox for "Yes, I agree."

  2. Under the question "How did you hear about us?", select "Referral" from the dropdown menu.

  3. Complete the "I'm not a robot" CAPTCHA.

  4. Click Submit.

17

MO to approve the application in Payex Terminal under “Merchant Application” Tab and click “Starter”

18

MO to click “save” as the MDR has already set to only FPX

19

Application approved on Payex

Submission to Paynet

20

Zian performs bulk submission to PayNet and cc's the Ops email for visibility.

Zian/Paynet

21

PayNet processes and returns credentials to Zian, who shares them to Keeper.

22

MO extracts credentials from Keeper and activates via Channel Activation Retool.

MO

FPX Switcher for Local MY Entity

  • By default, all live local MY merchants are instantly activated for FPX via the Aggregator MID without requiring a specific request.

  • MO monitors transaction activity via Databricks to identify accounts ready for submission to PayNet to receive their own dedicated Switcher MID.

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

Databricks Monitoring

1

Monitor Databrick Centre for transacting merchants ready for FPX Switcher (own MID).

2

Run the specific SQL script (provided below) to pull a list of active merchants:

select biz.id, biz.email, biz.name, biz.created_at, biz.updated_at, txn.MinDate, round(txn.tpv, 2) as tpv, txn.Count

from main.clean__xendit_kyc_service.business biz join (

select MID, MIN(date) AS MinDate, MAX(date) AS MaxDate, SUM(TPV) AS TPV, COUNT(*) AS Count

from main.transform__business_intelligence_revenue.malaysia_revenue_calculation

where date >= date_trunc('day', add_months(current_date, -6) + (current_date - date_trunc('day', current_date)))

AND product_type = 'DIRECT_DEBIT'

GROUP BY MID

) AS txn ON biz.id = txn.MID

where biz.email not like '%@xendit.co' and biz.id in

(

select routing_rule.business_id as bid

from `main`.`clean__payment_settings`.`merchant_routing_rules` routing_rule

where status = 'ACTIVE'

and pm_type = 'DIRECT_DEBIT'

and connection_id in (

'96172e7b-8912-4629-b920-5a42c1766e7a',

'7e738a54-2f08-4300-8205-794021e8c099'

)

group by routing_rule.business_id

limit 500

);

Example:

3

From the results, collect the data of the merchant and proceed submission to Payex and Paynet

Submission to Payex

4

MO fills FPX Submission Tracker (Columns to E). Use dummy email suffix in column C: malaysia.(tradename)@xendit.co

MO

5

The MO team will then manually submit the application to Payex. This is done using the Xendit Reference sign up link URL, utilizing the merchant details previously recorded in the FPX Submission Tracker & Spread Sheet.

6

For the Login Email and Confirm Login Email fields, please input the dummy email located in Column C. The format should follow: malaysia.(tradename)@xendit.co.

7

Always choose “Starter” Plan

8

Choose “FPX” for mode of payment

9

Then, fill the business details with (following the details in FPX Submission Tracker):

  • Type of Business Entity

  • Nature of Business

  • Registered Business Name

  • Trade Name

  • Business Registration Number

  • Registered Company Address

  • Business or Operation Address

10

After filling in all the merchant business details, MO will proceed to the next page to enter the 'Size of Company'

11

MO must then complete the Director Information section. Please ensure the Director Name is entered exactly as it appears on their NRIC (Malaysian ID).

You will also need to provide the NRIC number and Phone Number, following the specific details listed in the FPX Submission Tracker.

12

Next, MO needs to complete the Contact Person (CP) section. If the contact person is the same as the director, simply tick the checkbox for 'Same as Director’s Details.'

However, if the contact person is different, please input the appropriate information as specified in the FPX Submission Tracker..

13

MO will need to enter all relevant business information, including the merchant’s Website and Social Media handles. Additionally, please ensure to include the merchant's e-commerce URL (if available) to strengthen the application. All details should be cross-referenced with the information provided in the FPX Submission Tracker.

14

MO must then upload the merchant’s Legal Documents to verify their business identity. These files can be extracted directly from the Admin dashboard. Please ensure all documents are clear and legible before submitting, as per the details in the FPX Submission Tracker.

15

MO must then complete the Settlement Details using the following information:

  • Bank Name: United Overseas Bank

  • Bank Account Name: Xendit Malaysia Sdn Bhd

  • Bank Account Number: 2203042770

  • Notification Email: malaysia.(merchant name)@xendit.co

  • Confirm Notification Email: malaysia.(merchant name)@xendit.co

16

For the Notification Email and Confirm Notification Email, please use the exact same dummy email created during the first step (Login Email):

  • Format: malaysia.(merchant name)@xendit.co

17

In cases where the bank statement is not available in AD, MO may upload a blank page as a placeholder to proceed with the application.

This ensures the mandatory field is satisfied when Xendit’s internal data is being used for settlement instead.

18

Final Submission Steps:

  1. Tick the checkbox for "Yes, I agree."

  2. Under the question "How did you hear about us?", select "Referral" from the dropdown menu.

  3. Complete the "I'm not a robot" CAPTCHA.

  4. Click Submit.

19

MO to approve the application in Payex Terminal under “Merchant Application” Tab and click “Starter”

20

MO to click “save” as the MDR has already set to only FPX

21

Application approved on Payex

Submission to Paynet

22

Zian performs bulk submission to PayNet and cc's the Ops email for visibility.

Zian/Paynet

23

PayNet processes and returns credentials to Zian, who shares them to Keeper.

24

MO extracts credentials from Keeper and activates via Channel Activation Retool.

MO

Switcher Configuration

25

Once credentials in Keeper is ready, MO needs to delete the old Aggregator MID rules using FPX Routing Rule Remover Retool

Example:

Activation Flow

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

Channel Activation

1

MO should extract the necessary credentials from Keeper to perform the activation within the Channel Activation Retool.

MO

2

Select the Channel Activation Tab.
Enter the merchant BID, and set the transacting entity to Payex PLT

3

Set the Payment Method to Direct Debit and the Channel to All FPX Channels, then click Next.

4

Input the Email and Secret Key (retrieved from Keeper) and click Next.

5

Review the activation preview log for any errors. If everything looks correct, click Activate.

Post-Activation & Reporting:

6

After the activation, MO needs to fill in the Activation Date in Columns H & I of the FPX Submission Tracker.

7

MO to reply to the original thread in #mo-request stating that the merchant’s FPX is now activated and the process is complete.

Rejection Flow

There is no rejection flow for the FPX switcher. As long as the merchant has successfully passed the KYC process, they are automatically eligible for FPX. MO may proceed with the activation for all KYC-approved merchants without expecting a rejection for this specific payment channel.

GROUP BY MID

) AS txn ON biz.id = txn.MID

where biz.email not like '%@xendit.co' and biz.id in

(

select routing_rule.business_id as bid

from `main`.`clean__payment_settings`.`merchant_routing_rules` routing_rule

where status = 'ACTIVE'

and pm_type = 'DIRECT_DEBIT'

and connection_id in (

'96172e7b-8912-4629-b920-5a42c1766e7a',

'7e738a54-2f08-4300-8205-794021e8c099'

)

group by routing_rule.business_id

limit 500

);

Example:

3

From the results, collect the data of the merchant and proceed submission to Payex and Paynet

Submission to Payex

4

MO fills FPX Submission Tracker (Columns to E). Use dummy email suffix in column C: malaysia.(tradename)@xendit.co

MO

5

The MO team will then manually submit the application to Payex. This is done using the Xendit Reference sign up link URL, utilizing the merchant details previously recorded in the FPX Submission Tracker & Spread Sheet.

6

For the Login Email and Confirm Login Email fields, please input the dummy email located in Column C. The format should follow: malaysia.(tradename)@xendit.co.

7

Always choose “Starter” Plan

8

Choose “FPX” for mode of payment

9

Then, fill the business details with (following the details in FPX Submission Tracker):

  • Type of Business Entity
  • Nature of Business
  • Registered Business Name
  • Trade Name
  • Business Registration Number
  • Registered Company Address
  • Business or Operation Address

10

After filling in all the merchant business details, MO will proceed to the next page to enter the 'Size of Company'

11

MO must then complete the Director Information section. Please ensure the Director Name is entered exactly as it appears on their NRIC (Malaysian ID).

You will also need to provide the NRIC number and Phone Number, following the specific details listed in the FPX Submission Tracker.

12

Next, MO needs to complete the Contact Person (CP) section. If the contact person is the same as the director, simply tick the checkbox for 'Same as Director’s Details.'

However, if the contact person is different, please input the appropriate information as specified in the FPX Submission Tracker..

13

MO will need to enter all relevant business information, including the merchant’s Website and Social Media handles. Additionally, please ensure to include the merchant's e-commerce URL (if available) to strengthen the application. All details should be cross-referenced with the information provided in the FPX Submission Tracker.

14

MO must then upload the merchant’s Legal Documents to verify their business identity. These files can be extracted directly from the Admin dashboard. Please ensure all documents are clear and legible before submitting, as per the details in the FPX Submission Tracker.

15

MO must then complete the Settlement Details using the following information:

  • Bank Name: United Overseas Bank
  • Bank Account Name: Xendit Malaysia Sdn Bhd
  • Bank Account Number: 2203042770
  • Notification Email: malaysia.(merchant name)@xendit.co
  • Confirm Notification Email: malaysia.(merchant name)@xendit.co

16

For the Notification Email and Confirm Notification Email, please use the exact same dummy email created during the first step (Login Email):

  • Format: malaysia.(merchant name)@xendit.co

17

In cases where the bank statement is not available in AD, MO may upload a blank page as a placeholder to proceed with the application.

This ensures the mandatory field is satisfied when Xendit’s internal data is being used for settlement instead.

18

Final Submission Steps:

  1. Tick the checkbox for "Yes, I agree."
  2. Under the question "How did you hear about us?", select "Referral" from the dropdown menu.
  3. Complete the "I'm not a robot" CAPTCHA.
  4. Click Submit.

19

MO to approve the application in Payex Terminal under “Merchant Application” Tab and click “Starter”

20

MO to click “save” as the MDR has already set to only FPX

21

Application approved on Payex

Submission to Paynet

22

Zian performs bulk submission to PayNet and cc's the Ops email for visibility.

Zian/Paynet

23

PayNet processes and returns credentials to Zian, who shares them to Keeper.

24

MO extracts credentials from Keeper and activates via Channel Activation Retool.

MO

Switcher Configuration

25

Once credentials in Keeper is ready, MO needs to delete the old Aggregator MID rules using FPX Routing Rule Remover Retool

Example:

Activation Flow

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

Channel Activation

1

MO should extract the necessary credentials from Keeper to perform the activation within the Channel Activation Retool.

MO

2

Select the Channel Activation Tab.
Enter the merchant BID, and set the transacting entity to Payex PLT

3

Set the Payment Method to Direct Debit and the Channel to All FPX Channels, then click Next.

4

Input the Email and Secret Key (retrieved from Keeper) and click Next.

5

Review the activation preview log for any errors. If everything looks correct, click Activate.

Post-Activation & Reporting:

6

After the activation, MO needs to fill in the Activation Date in Columns H & I of the FPX Submission Tracker.

7

MO to reply to the original thread in #mo-request stating that the merchant’s FPX is now activated and the process is complete.

Rejection Flow

There is no rejection flow for the FPX switcher. As long as the merchant has successfully passed the KYC process, they are automatically eligible for FPX. MO may proceed with the activation for all KYC-approved merchants without expecting a rejection for this specific payment channel.

  • When ready, MO submits them to PayNet for their own Switcher MID.

MO

FPX Switcher for Local MY Entity

  • By default, all live local MY merchants are instantly activated for FPX via the Aggregator MID without requiring a specific request.
  • MO monitors transaction activity via Databricks to identify accounts ready for submission to PayNet to receive their own dedicated Switcher MID.

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

Databricks Monitoring

1

Monitor Databrick Centre for transacting merchants ready for FPX Switcher (own MID).

2

Run the specific SQL script (provided below) to pull a list of active merchants:

select biz.id, biz.email, biz.name, biz.created_at, biz.updated_at, txn.MinDate, round(txn.tpv, 2) as tpv, txn.Count

from main.clean__xendit_kyc_service.business biz join (

select MID, MIN(date) AS MinDate, MAX(date) AS MaxDate, SUM(TPV) AS TPV, COUNT(*) AS Count

from main.transform__business_intelligence_revenue.malaysia_revenue_calculation

where date >= date_trunc('day', add_months(current_date, -6) + (current_date - date_trunc('day', current_date)))

AND product_type = 'DIRECT_DEBIT'

GROUP BY MID

) AS txn ON biz.id = txn.MID

where biz.email not like '%@xendit.co' and biz.id in

(

select routing_rule.business_id as bid

from `main`.`clean__payment_settings`.`merchant_routing_rules` routing_rule

where status = 'ACTIVE'

and pm_type = 'DIRECT_DEBIT'

and connection_id in (

'96172e7b-8912-4629-b920-5a42c1766e7a',

'7e738a54-2f08-4300-8205-794021e8c099'

)

group by routing_rule.business_id

limit 500

);
